title: Surveyor: automatic meta-improvement loop for both engines (LLM + gobot)
description:
Admiral requirement (2026-07-06): meta-improvement must be AUTOMATIC; harbormaster is manual-only and the wrong home. Design:

(1) SURVEYOR - new standing city agent (city/agents/surveyor, opus), the marine-surveyor of the operation: review-only, never edits anything. Woken by the WATCHKEEPER on a cadence (config captain.meta_review_days=7, or every N captain sessions - whichever first). Reads: engine report + decision beads (outcome-completion sample), captain_events ack latency, consult answer latency, engine-labeled friction, session transcripts token costs, template-vs-practice drift. OUTPUT: engine-labeled bug/feature beads with evidence into the shipwright queue + one-screen digest mail to Admiral. Structural independence: separate session+template from the captain (self-review drift blindness was why old metareview.go existed; it was deleted at cutover with no true replacement - this is the successor, OUTSIDE the thing it reviews).

(2) ENGINE REPORT - small read-only verb (spacetraders engine report [--json]): wakes/day, tokens/wake (transcript parse - dashboard token_stats logic exists), event ack latency, decisions opened vs closed-with-outcome, consults answered vs pending, rollover count. Gives the surveyor numbers instead of vibes.

(3) WATCHKEEPER wiring - nudge surveyor on cadence; DISABLED kill switch structurally silences it like everything else. Tier 3 (watchkeeper touch).

(4) LABEL CONVENTION - captain/crew templates gain one line: engine-friction files with label 'engine' (distinct from fleet friction). Tier 3 (template edit).

(5) LOOP CLOSURE - surveyor beads flow the EXISTING tiers: Tier 1/2 (gobot-side: detectors, verbs, report improvements) fully automatic behind the gate; Tier 3 (templates/watchkeeper/gate) queues for Admiral sign-off. Deliberate ceiling: the engine never gets autonomy over its own cage - automatic UP TO the approval line. Era retrospective (archive spec section 4.4) remains the deep per-universe review; surveyor is the weekly shallow loop.

BUILD: blocked until archive-reset workflow lands (file collision: internal/captain + config). Admiral order = Tier-3 sign-off; recommend building on fresh pool.
notes:
Name rationale: marine surveyor - inspects vessels for seaworthiness, reports to owners, repairs are the shipwright's job. Exactly the division of labor.
Track A LAUNCHED now per Admiral (collision analysis: only watchkeeper wiring conflicts with in-flight archive build): (1) surveyor template authored (opus), (2) engine report v1 under captain cmd group - captain_events metrics only, beads/token metrics deferred with noted boundary, (3) FABLE reviews+fixes ALL 6 templates against post-cutover ground truth incl new verbs, consult mail+nudge, engine-label convention. Track B REMAINS: watchkeeper cadence wiring (supervisor.go+config) after archive build lands.
Track A COMMITTED: surveyor template (fable-reviewed), captain report v1, all 6 templates audited+fixed by fable (shipwright DISABLED gap closed, harbormaster drift healed, cross-template protocol consistency enforced, renders verified). Track B (watchkeeper cadence wiring) remains - blocked on archive build landing.
close_reason: FULLY BUILT+COMMITTED across tracks A+B: surveyor template (fable-reviewed), captain report v1 telemetry, all-6-template fable audit, watchkeeper cadence wiring (meta_review_days, spawn-if-dead, mail+nudge, 0-disabled end-to-end after review repair). The loop: watchkeeper wakes surveyor weekly -> surveyor reviews both engines vs telemetry+beads -> files engine-labeled beads -> shipwright builds behind the gate (Tier 3 -> Admiral). Deferred, noted: cadence-by-sessions variant; beads/token metrics in report v2; surveyor probe-error edge (2 MINOR review notes on the bead trail).

title: Cross-universe game archive: preserve reset universes as a learnable history corpus
description:
Admiral requirement (2026-07-06): on universe reset, do NOT just wipe - ARCHIVE each universe's history so cross-era data improves design + play. Reframes scout feature #8 from wipe-hygiene to archive-then-reset.

WHAT'S CROSS-UNIVERSE (archive + query): transactions/P&L, contract economics (which goods/types paid), market_price_history (GOOD-level volatility/supply behaviour repeats across universes even as maps differ - cf lesson l18 fuel-is-volatile), manufacturing chain profitability, captain_events. Qualitative half (decisions/lessons/strategy) ALREADY cross-universe + persistent in the sp- beads ledger post-bridge - it just accumulates eras.
WHAT'S PER-UNIVERSE (wipe): map (systems/waypoints/coords), ship instances, the gate, active operations/pipelines.

RECOMMENDED SHAPE: era-stamped archive tables (or schema) populated from live rows on reset BEFORE truncating the map/fleet tables; era key = universe/agent/faction + reset timestamp. Plus a read verb (spacetraders history goods --good X / history contracts) so the captain + Trade-Analyst prime the new universe with priors at cold-start.
ALTERNATIVES: full pg_dump per era (simple backup, NOT queryable-live for priming); universe_id column on live tables never-deleted (unbounded + poison-risk if a filter is forgotten - rejected).
PREREQUISITE to st-wm7 new-universe bring-up: archive must run before any live-table reset. Consumers: design-time cross-era analysis (Admiral), play-time priors (captain/analysts at cold-start).
notes:
BEADS-LAYER DESIGN (corrects the earlier 'beads are cross-universe for free' framing - they're a MIX):

UNIVERSE-INDEPENDENT (persist untouched across resets, no era): code-improvement features (scout backlog), bugs, the Shipwright queue, and UNIVERSAL lessons ('fuel is volatile', 'frozen ledger is the alarm'). These are about the BOT, not the game state.

UNIVERSE-SCOPED (era-label + archive on reset): tactical decisions (nearly all - they name specific ships/waypoints/the gate), universe-specific lessons ('D45 is the only ADV_CIRCUITRY exporter'), and the living strategy bead. Design: label era:<name>; on reset bulk-close the OPEN ones (they stay queryable as history: bd list -l era:torwind --status closed), start a fresh era. Retrospective across closed eras seeds the new universe's fresh strategy + priors.

SHARPEST RISK - MEMORIES: bd remember memories are AUTO-PRIMED into every session. A universe-specific memory would prime a fresh captain with FALSE priors. Reset needs a memory-review gate: keep universal, retire/era-tag universe-specific.

IMMEDIATE CONSEQUENCE (state I just created): the migration imported 216 TORWIND (universe-1) decisions + 61 lessons into the live sp- ledger, currently UN-tagged. Before st-wm7 brings up universe-2, these must be era:torwind-tagged and the open decisions closed, and the strategy bead sp-s2q demoted to a retrospective seed - else the universe-2 captain reads universe-1 tactics as current. The reset process, not the migration, does this.
FULL DESIGN SPEC written + committed: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-06-cross-universe-archive-reset-design.md (520 lines, reviewed by harbormaster - classification + runbook read in full, sound). This is the design of record. Shipwright Tier 3 (schema + destructive verb + Watchkeeper + templates -> Admiral sign-off), 7-step gated build order in the spec. AutoMigrate structural fix (AllModels registry) is step 1.
SPEC AMENDED to rev 2 per Admiral challenge: in-place player-partitioned history (player_id IS the era key; verified 13/17 models carry it). No archive schema/copying; eras registry + waypoints.era_id only; universe close+scrub replace archive/verify/truncate; runbook 9 phases. Build order in spec updated (step 1 AllModels already shipped). Still Tier 3, still awaiting Admiral build sign-off.
Admiral: BUILD WITH WORKFLOW (2026-07-06) = Tier-3 sign-off on rev 2. Launching: foundations (eras+era_id+GetServerStatus+status verb, opus) -> parallel lanes close/scrub (opus), history verbs+CI guard (sonnet/opus), watchkeeper reset detector (opus craft, FABLE review - kill-switch rail), register --new (opus), runbook+era-close helper (sonnet) -> full gate.
close_reason: BUILT+COMMITTED (rev 2, in-place player-partitioned): eras registry, waypoints.era_id, universe status/close/scrub, 7 history verbs, reset detector, register --new, runbook + era-close helper. Execution of the runbook itself = next session (st-wm7).

description:
# Standing strategy

## Standing Admiral directives
**#1 FABRICATION PATH (s71, Admiral order #1).** The jump-gate bill (FAB_MATS 1600 + ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY 400 at
X1-PZ28-I67) and the manufacturing income stream run on the SAME engine: `operations start --system X1-PZ28
--manufacturing` (discovers high-demand goods, prefer-fabricate resolver) + targeted `goods produce <GOOD> --system
X1-PZ28`. PREMISE CORRECTED s86 (L58): both goods are NOW sold in-system — our own factories woke the exports
(FAB_MATS @F56 532 ABUNDANT, ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY @D45 1,900 MODERATE) → depth-3 buy-final IS available, bill ≈1.6–2M.
GAS OPS EVALUATED s86 (Admiral ask): IMMATERIAL — 0% of fabrication inputs are siphonable (gases only enter recipes
via EXPLOSIVES, never reached; resolver buys ore/sand/crystal leaves) and gases cost 23–28 at C42 anyway. CLOSED;
plumbing (STORAGE_ACQUIRE_DELIVER, cost-0) exists if a gas-consuming chain ever matters. Manufacturing auto-claims idle light
haulers with NO exclusion flag, BUT ship ASSIGNMENTS already give mutual exclusion (a claim writes
`assignment_status='active'` → hidden from every other coordinator), so a manufacturing/construction hauler is
auto-excluded from contracts with NO flag needed (reservation flag REJECTED as phantom, reverted 6fee4f1).
X1-PZ28 has a real prefer-fabricate thesis (cheap ores J70/B7 → SCARCE high-value finished goods A1) but sell
volumes 6–20 SCARCE/WEAK cap standalone $/h (L13). `goods produce` has NO --dry-run (friction).

## KPI targets
- **Credits/hour: baseline ~21,900/hr; ACTUAL ~216k/hr (~9.9× KPI, s86), climbing since manufacturing went live.** The binding
  constraint on the EARNER is CYCLE TIME, not contract supply (d-35/L48): contract EXECUTION = ~67% of wall-clock
  is travel; cadence is endogenous (coordinator negotiates next contract on fulfillment). A 2nd hauler cut mean
  buy-leg distance via the "select closest ship" balancer → more cycles/hour. Coordinator runs ONE contract at a
  time (L45) — extra haulers add positioning, not parallelism. NET per mega-contract ~+174k at ~67–73% margin
  (L45). Watch margin erosion (rising cargo cost vs payout) as the saturation signal (L13).
- Fleet utilization: no ship idle > 60 min without a recorded reason.

## Horizon plan (Admiral challenge, s53 d-60) — mission beyond credits/hour
**Jump gate is the mission spine; idle capital is NOT the constraint — TOOLING/DEFECTS are.** Treasury ~3.27M

title: friction: (s85) `construction start` is a SILENT FOOTGUN: it creates the pipeline row (pipeline_type=CONSTRUCTION) + tas
description: friction: (s85) `construction start` is a SILENT FOOTGUN: it creates the pipeline row (pipeline_type=CONSTRUCTION) + tasks and returns "Status: PLANNING", but spawns NO coordinator container and returns NO container id — the pipeline sits INERT at 0.0% forever with nothing executing it. The executor is the MANUFACTURING coordinator, which adopts pipelines ONLY at startup recovery (`FindByStatus(PLANNING,EXECUTING)`, `run_parallel_manufacturing_coordinator.go` recoverState at ~line 200) and NEVER re-polls the table in its main loop. So a construction pipeline created AFTER the coordinator booted is never seen. The undocumented required sequence is: `construction start` FIRST, THEN bounce `operations --manufacturing` so recovery adopts it. Candidate fix: either (a) `construction start` should signal the running coordinator to re-scan (or spawn its own coordinator), or (b) the coordinator loop should periodically re-poll for new PLANNING pipelines, or at minimum (c) `construction start` should WARN "no coordinator running — start operations --manufacturing to execute this pipeline". Nothing about the success output hints the pipeline is inert.

title: friction: (s85) No way to PRIORITIZE mission/gate (CONSTRUCTION) tasks over income (manufacturing) tasks — the manufactu
description: friction: (s85) No way to PRIORITIZE mission/gate (CONSTRUCTION) tasks over income (manufacturing) tasks — the manufacturing coordinator runs a UNIFIED task queue across both pipeline types with a shared `--max-workers` pool and no per-pipeline worker pinning or priority. A large manufacturing backlog (10-14 queued COLLECT_SELL tasks) can starve the gate-build tasks of workers even though the gate is the higher mission priority. Candidate fix: a per-pipeline-type priority/weight, or a `construction start --dedicate <ship>` that pins a hauler, so mission-critical construction isn't forced to compete on equal footing with income tasks.

title: friction: (s75) THE FIX-PIPELINE GATE (build+tests) IS BLIND TO A COORDINATOR THAT CRASHES THE WHOLE DAEMON ON LAUNCH —
description: friction: (s75) THE FIX-PIPELINE GATE (build+tests) IS BLIND TO A COORDINATOR THAT CRASHES THE WHOLE DAEMON ON LAUNCH — same class as the s73 missing-migration blind spot. `operations start --manufacturing` panics the daemon in a restart loop because the parallel-manufacturing handler's `eventSubscriber`/`eventPublisher` are never wired in main.go (they're nil), and the command runs on a naked goroutine with no recover() (L53, report d-83). description: friction: (s73) THE FIX-PIPELINE GATE CANNOT CATCH A MISSING-MIGRATION REGRESSION. The build+test gate is structurally blind to this class. CANDIDATE FIX (high value — this outage cost hours of zero income): add a schema-drift check to the gate that compares GORM model tags against the applied `migrations/` DDL (or runs the production migration path in CI) and FAILS a schema-touching feature that lacks its migration. Second-order gap: the Captain has no way to apply a DB migration (CLI-only actuator, no psql) — a migration fix requires an out-of-band operator step even after code merges, so schema fixes have a longer restore-latency than pure-code fixes; a `spacetraders admin migrate` verb (or auto-run migrations on daemon boot) would let the daemon self-repair schema drift on restart.
